data.title: Standard Life executives exercise incentive plan options and sell shares
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Phoenix Group Holdings ( (GB:SDLF) ) has shared an update.

Standard Life has disclosed that Group Chief Executive Officer Andrew Briggs and Group General Counsel Quentin Zentner have exercised nil-cost options over ordinary shares under the company’s Long Term Incentive Plan and Deferred Bonus Share Scheme. The options, which vested after performance and holding periods tied to financial years up to 2023, resulted in Briggs retaining a portion of shares while selling others to meet tax and national insurance liabilities, whereas Zentner sold all shares acquired.

The transactions, conducted on 26 March 2026 on the London Stock Exchange at around 670 pence per share, form part of Standard Life’s routine executive remuneration and incentive structure. The disclosure, made under UK Market Abuse Regulation requirements, provides transparency on senior management equity dealings but does not signal any change in corporate strategy or governance beyond normal incentive plan operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:SDLF) stock is a Sell with a £6.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Phoenix Group Holdings stock, see the GB:SDLF Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SDLF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SDLF is a Neutral.

Phoenix Group Holdings’ overall stock score is driven by strong earnings call results and positive corporate events, which highlight strategic progress and financial resilience. However, the mixed financial performance and valuation concerns due to ongoing profitability challenges temper the overall score. The technical analysis suggests a bullish trend, further supporting the stock’s potential.

To see Spark’s full report on SDLF stock, click here.

More about Phoenix Group Holdings

Standard Life plc is a UK-based savings, investments and retirement company, providing long-term savings products, pensions and related financial services. Listed on the London Stock Exchange, it operates in the broader life insurance and asset management space, serving retail and institutional clients seeking retirement and investment solutions.

Average Trading Volume: 2,213,719

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £6.71B

data.title: British Smaller Companies VCT2 Buys Back Shares, Updates Voting Rights
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

British Smaller Companies VCT2 ( (GB:BSC) ) has issued an announcement.

British Smaller Companies VCT2 plc has repurchased 2,484,587 ordinary shares, representing 0.72% of its prior voting rights, at 52.18 pence per share, and will hold these shares in treasury. Following the transaction, the company’s issued share capital with voting rights stands at 342,471,654 ordinary shares, while it holds 38,696,133 shares in treasury, with the updated voting-share figure serving as the new reference point for investors’ disclosure obligations under FCA transparency rules.

The last reported net asset value was 54.40 pence per share as of 31 December 2025, indicating the buyback was executed at a modest discount to NAV. This buyback may be seen as part of the trust’s ongoing capital management strategy, potentially supporting the share price and providing clarity for shareholders on current voting rights and regulatory reporting thresholds.

More about British Smaller Companies VCT2

British Smaller Companies VCT2 plc is a UK-listed venture capital trust that invests in a portfolio of smaller British companies, providing growth capital and tax-efficient exposure for investors. The trust’s shares trade on the London market, and it actively manages its capital structure through share issuance and buyback activity.

Average Trading Volume: 4,697

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

An update from Downing ONE VCT ( (GB:FVEN) ) is now available.

Foresight Ventures VCT plc has allotted 301,023 new ordinary shares at 88.4p each under its Dividend Reinvestment Scheme, allowing shareholders to reinvest their dividends into additional equity in the trust. This move modestly increases the company’s share capital to 105,954,684 ordinary shares in issue and underscores ongoing investor engagement with the VCT as it continues to deploy capital into its portfolio of smaller companies.

The newly issued shares have been submitted for admission to the UK Listing Authority’s Official List and to trading on the London Stock Exchange, with admission expected on or around 31 March 2026. The issuance via the DRIS supports the trust’s capital base without a separate fundraising, potentially enhancing liquidity in the stock while maintaining alignment between the VCT and its income-focused shareholder base.

More about Downing ONE VCT

Foresight Ventures VCT plc is a UK-listed venture capital trust focused on investing in smaller, growth-oriented companies, providing investors with exposure to a diversified portfolio of venture and growth capital opportunities. The trust’s shares trade on the London Stock Exchange, giving retail and institutional investors access to its venture investment strategy.

Samhi Hotels Limited ( (IN:SAMHI) ) has issued an update.

Samhi Hotels Limited has increased its equity base by allotting 928,582 new shares with a face value of ₹1 each, following the exercise of options granted under its Employees Stock Option Plan 2023. The newly issued shares, which have received in-principle approvals from both BSE and NSE, carry no lock-in period and rank pari passu with existing equity shares.

With this allotment, the company’s issued, subscribed, and paid-up share capital has risen from ₹22.12 crore to ₹22.21 crore, reflecting a modest dilution in equity to accommodate employee ownership. The move underscores Samhi Hotels’ use of share-based incentives to retain and reward talent, aligning employee interests with long-term shareholder value in a competitive hospitality market.

Platinex ( (TSE:PTX) ) just unveiled an update.

PTX Metals has secured $215,000 in funding under the Ontario Junior Exploration Program, providing support for eligible mineral exploration and development costs as it advances key critical minerals projects in Northern Ontario. The company framed the award as part of a wider policy push by Ontario and federal authorities to bolster domestic mineral supply, in particular around the Ring of Fire region.

The release highlights a series of recent government commitments to infrastructure, Indigenous partnerships, processing capacity, and critical minerals-focused funds that together underscore growing alignment between policymakers and industry. For PTX, this evolving support landscape is expected to facilitate continued exploration of its W2 copper-nickel-PGE asset and reinforces the strategic importance of responsible development to Canada’s economic security and supply chain resilience.

Platinex’s stock is heavily impacted by its poor financial performance, with zero revenue generation and ongoing operational losses. While there is some technical momentum suggesting short-term positivity, the high risk of being overbought and negative valuation metrics further dampen the stock’s attractiveness.

More about Platinex

PTX Metals Inc. is a Toronto-based junior mining and exploration company focused on advancing critical mineral projects in Ontario, including its flagship W2 copper-nickel-PGE project near the Ring of Fire. The company targets metals essential to emerging clean energy, electrification, and defence supply chains, positioning itself within Canada’s broader critical minerals strategy.

An announcement from StrongPoint ASA ( (DE:PGT) ) is now available.

StrongPoint ASA has published its annual financial and audit report for 2025, making the documentation available in European Single Electronic Format and PDF via its website. The release underscores the company’s compliance with Norwegian securities disclosure rules, providing investors and other stakeholders with updated, standardized financial information that supports transparency and market assessment of the grocery tech specialist’s performance.

The company’s latest reporting package comes as StrongPoint continues to position itself as a key technology partner for grocery and retail chains seeking more efficient e-commerce and in-store operations. By offering detailed, accessible annual reporting, the Oslo-listed group reinforces its governance standards and gives the market a clearer basis for evaluating its strategy and execution in the rapidly evolving retail technology segment.

More about StrongPoint ASA

StrongPoint ASA is a Norway-headquartered grocery retail technology company that develops solutions to make brick-and-mortar shops smarter, improve in-store shopping, and streamline online grocery operations. With roughly 500 employees across the Nordics, the Baltics, Spain, the UK and Ireland, and a broad partner network, it serves grocery and retail clients in more than 20 countries and generates about NOK 1.4 billion in annual revenue from end-to-end e-commerce systems and in-store technologies.

data.title: Hoist Finance Sets 7 May 2026 Date for AGM, Proposes New Share Programs and Capital Mandates
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hoist Finance AB ( (SE:HOFI) ) has issued an update.

Hoist Finance AB has convened its Annual General Meeting for 7 May 2026 in Stockholm, inviting shareholders registered with Euroclear Sweden by 28 April and properly notified by 30 April to participate. Shareholders with nominee-registered shares must temporarily re-register them in their own names to exercise voting rights at the meeting.

The AGM agenda includes approval of the 2025 financial statements, decisions on profit allocation, discharge of liability for the board and CEO, and resolutions on board and auditor elections and remuneration. Investors will also vote on new share investment programs for employees and board members and authorisations for the board to issue new shares and repurchase own shares, measures that could influence capital structure, incentives, and governance for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:HOFI) stock is a Hold with a SEK167.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hoist Finance AB stock, see the SE:HOFI Stock Forecast page.

More about Hoist Finance AB

Hoist Finance AB is a Swedish publicly listed financial services company focused on acquiring and managing non-performing and performing consumer and corporate loans. Operating primarily in European credit markets, it works with banks and financial institutions to service and recover distressed assets, positioning itself as a specialist in credit management and portfolio investments.

data.title: OFS Capital Amends Revolving Credit Facility for Flexibility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

OFS Capital ( (OFS) ) has provided an update.

On March 27, 2026, OFS Capital Corporation amended its senior secured revolving credit facility with Banc of California, which is used for general corporate purposes including funding new investments. The amendment reduces covenant pressure by lowering the minimum tangible net asset value requirement from $100 million to $75 million and temporarily cutting the minimum quarterly net investment income after management and incentive fees from $2 million to $1 million for the quarters ending March 31, June 30 and September 30, 2026.

At the same time, OFS Capital reduced the maximum commitment on the facility from $25 million to $15 million, signaling a smaller but more flexible borrowing base aligned with its current capital needs. The company incurred customary fees and expenses related to closing the amendment, which collectively adjusts its leverage and income thresholds in a way that may provide short-term operational flexibility while constraining total available liquidity.

The most recent analyst rating on (OFS) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on OFS Capital stock, see the OFS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OFS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OFS is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by sharply weaker TTM profitability and very weak technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting factors include improved leverage and solid recent cash generation, plus a supportive (but high-risk) income profile from the very high dividend yield and balance-sheet-focused actions highlighted on the latest earnings call.

To see Spark’s full report on OFS stock, click here.

More about OFS Capital

OFS Capital Corporation is a Delaware-based business development company that provides financing solutions to middle-market companies. Its primary activities include originating and managing a portfolio of debt and equity investments, with a focus on generating net investment income and total returns for its shareholders through a senior secured lending strategy.

data.title: CNT Group Narrows Annual Loss Despite Revenue Slump and Deeper Property Revaluation Hit
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CNT Group Limited ( (HK:0701) ) has shared an announcement.

CNT Group Limited, a Hong Kong-listed industrial and property investment group, reported weaker top-line performance for the year ended 31 December 2025 amid a challenging operating environment. The company continues to balance its paints and coatings operations with a portfolio of investment properties, leaving overall results sensitive to demand conditions and fair value movements.

Revenue fell 22.6% year-on-year to HK$267.7 million, with gross profit down 18.4% to HK$118.0 million, though the gross margin improved to 44.1% from 41.8%. The group narrowed its net loss to HK$66.7 million from HK$89.4 million, helped by lower selling and distribution expenses, reduced other expenses, and a reversal of impairment on trade and bills receivables.

A key drag on performance remained fair value losses on investment properties, which widened to HK$55.9 million from HK$46.6 million and continued to weigh heavily on the bottom line. Despite this, CNT Group’s cash and cash equivalents plus pledged deposits rose 6.9% to HK$330.2 million, while bank borrowings increased modestly to HK$141.4 million, keeping the gearing ratio relatively low at 11.8%.

Shareholders’ funds per share edged down to HK$0.63 from HK$0.64 and net asset value per share slipped to HK$0.68, reflecting cumulative losses and property revaluations. Basic and diluted loss per share improved to 2.90 Hong Kong cents from 4.06 cents, indicating some progress in loss containment even as the group remains in the red and exposed to ongoing property market volatility.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0701) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.18 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CNT Group Limited stock, see the HK:0701 Stock Forecast page.

More about CNT Group Limited

CNT Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda, with operations spanning paint manufacturing and property-related investments. The group generates revenue primarily from its coatings business while also holding investment properties, exposing it to both industrial demand cycles and fluctuations in real estate valuations.

Average Trading Volume: 195,886

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$335M

data.title: Ajisen China Proposes Final 2025 Dividend and Sets Payout Timetable
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ajisen (China) Holdings Limited ( (HK:0538) ) just unveiled an update.

Ajisen (China) Holdings Limited has proposed a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.07 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, payable in Hong Kong dollars at HKD 0.0791 per share based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.13. Subject to shareholder approval on 23 June 2026, the stock will trade ex-dividend on 25 June, with a record date of 2 July and payment scheduled for 14 August 2026, signalling continued capital returns to shareholders.

The dividend timetable sets the latest time to lodge share transfers on 26 June 2026 and a book closure period from 29 June to 2 July 2026, with no withholding tax or warrant-related adjustments applicable to this distribution. The announcement underscores the company’s ongoing dividend policy and provides investors with clear visibility on cash returns, which may be viewed as a sign of operational stability in a competitive restaurant sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0538) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ajisen (China) Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0538 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ajisen (China) Holdings Limited

Ajisen (China) Holdings Limited operates in the catering and restaurant industry, running noodle restaurant chains under the Ajisen brand across mainland China and related markets. The company focuses on casual dining and mass-market consumers, offering Japanese-style ramen and related food and beverage products in urban commercial locations.
